questions.q: What does the abbreviation 'IIT' stand for?
questions.explanation: IIT stands for the Indian Institutes of Technology, India's premier engineering and technology institutes.

questions.q: Which was the first IIT to be established?
questions.explanation: IIT Kharagpur, established in 1951, was the first of the Indian Institutes of Technology.

questions.q: In which year was the first IIT founded?
questions.explanation: IIT Kharagpur, the first IIT, was founded in 1951.

questions.q: The IITs are best known primarily as institutes of which field?
questions.explanation: The IITs are India's elite engineering and technology institutes.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the original IIT Kharagpur located?
questions.explanation: IIT Kharagpur is located in the town of Kharagpur in West Bengal.

questions.q: The IITs are public institutes funded primarily by which entity?
questions.explanation: The IITs are autonomous public institutions funded by the Government of India.

questions.q: IIT Bombay is located in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: IIT Bombay is located in Mumbai (formerly Bombay), Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which highly competitive examination is the main route for undergraduate admission to the IITs?
questions.explanation: Undergraduate admission to the IITs is primarily through the Joint Entrance Examination (JEE), specifically JEE Advanced.

questions.q: The IITs are widely regarded as a global byword for what?
questions.explanation: The IITs are globally recognized as a byword for talent and high-quality technical education.

questions.q: Which type of academic degree is most associated with IIT undergraduate programmes?
questions.explanation: The flagship undergraduate degree at the IITs is the Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech).

questions.q: IIT Kharagpur was set up on the site of a former British detention camp known by what name?
questions.explanation: IIT Kharagpur's first campus occupied the buildings of the Hijli Detention Camp, a former British prison.

questions.q: Which special law gives the IITs their distinctive status as 'Institutes of National Importance'?
questions.explanation: The Institutes of Technology Act, 1961 declares the IITs Institutes of National Importance and governs them.

questions.q: Which IIT was established with assistance from the former Soviet Union (USSR)?
questions.explanation: IIT Bombay, founded in 1958, was set up with technical and financial assistance from the Soviet Union, channelled through UNESCO.

questions.q: Which country provided significant assistance, through a consortium of universities, in establishing IIT Kanpur?
questions.explanation: IIT Kanpur was established with help from a consortium of nine leading American universities under the Kanpur Indo-American Programme.

questions.q: Which IIT was set up with collaboration from West Germany?
questions.explanation: IIT Madras, founded in 1959, was established with technical and financial collaboration from West Germany.

questions.q: Co-founder of Sun Microsystems and a celebrated IIT alumnus, Vinod Khosla graduated from which IIT?
questions.explanation: Vinod Khosla, co-founder of Sun Microsystems, earned his undergraduate degree at IIT Delhi.

questions.q: Sundar Pichai, CEO of Alphabet (Google), completed his undergraduate engineering degree at which IIT?
questions.explanation: Sundar Pichai earned his degree in metallurgical engineering at IIT Kharagpur.

questions.q: The five 'original' IITs founded between 1951 and 1961 were Kharagpur, Bombay, Madras, Kanpur and which other?
questions.explanation: IIT Delhi, founded in 1961, completes the group of five original IITs alongside Kharagpur, Bombay, Madras and Kanpur.

questions.q: IIT Roorkee traces its origins to a 19th-century college, making its lineage among the oldest. What was it earlier known as?
questions.explanation: IIT Roorkee evolved from the Thomason College of Civil Engineering (later University of Roorkee), founded in 1847, and became an IIT in 2001.

questions.q: Which examination is primarily used for admission to IIT postgraduate (M.Tech) programmes and PSU recruitment?
questions.explanation: GATE, the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering, is the main gateway to IIT postgraduate programmes and is widely used for PSU hiring.

questions.q: Approximately how many IITs operate across India today (as of the mid-2020s)?
questions.explanation: There are 23 IITs across India as of the mid-2020s, expanded greatly from the original handful.

questions.q: Which IIT is located in the northeastern region of India?
questions.explanation: IIT Guwahati, in Assam, established in 1994, is the major IIT serving northeastern India.

questions.q: Who served as the first Director of IIT Kharagpur?
questions.explanation: Jnan Chandra (J. C.) Ghosh, a distinguished chemist, was the first Director of IIT Kharagpur.

questions.q: The Sarkar Committee, whose 1940s recommendations led to the IITs, was named after its chairman. Who was he?
questions.explanation: The committee chaired by Nalini Ranjan Sarkar recommended establishing higher technical institutions, leading to the IITs.

questions.q: In which year was IIT Kanpur formally established?
questions.explanation: IIT Kanpur was established in 1959, the same year as IIT Madras.

questions.q: The first computer science program in India was pioneered at IIT Kanpur in the 1960s with the installation of which early computer?
questions.explanation: IIT Kanpur installed an IBM 1620 in 1963 and ran pioneering computer-science courses, helping launch formal computing education in India.

questions.q: Raghuram Rajan, former Governor of the Reserve Bank of India and IMF chief economist, is an alumnus of which IIT?
questions.explanation: Raghuram Rajan earned his undergraduate engineering degree at IIT Delhi before pursuing economics.

questions.q: The IIT Council, the apex body coordinating all the IITs, is chaired ex officio by which official?
questions.explanation: The IIT Council is chaired by the Union Minister of Education (formerly Human Resource Development), who coordinates the IITs.

questions.q: Nandan Nilekani, co-founder of Infosys and architect of India's Aadhaar system, graduated from which IIT?
questions.explanation: Nandan Nilekani, Infosys co-founder and Aadhaar architect, is an electrical engineering graduate of IIT Bombay.

questions.q: IIT Madras runs a celebrated technology business incubator that has produced many startups. By what well-known name is its incubation cell known?
options: IIT Madras Research Park / Incubation Cell
questions.explanation: IIT Madras Research Park and its associated incubation cell are renowned for nurturing deep-tech startups.
